expectations.text: A test file exists and contains at least 4 tests covering plain number, multiple of 3, multiple of 5, and multiple of 15
expectations.evidence: test_fizzbuzz.py exists with 8 tests: plain numbers (1, 2), multiples of 3 (3, 6), multiples of 5 (5, 10), and multiples of 15 (15, 30). All four required cases are covered.

expectations.text: The production code file exists and all tests pass
expectations.evidence: fizzbuzz.py exists and all 8 tests pass: '8 passed in 0.02s' confirmed by running pytest.

expectations.text: The agent described or produced a test list before writing any code
expectations.evidence: Transcript '## Step 0 — Test List' section lists all 8 test cases before any production code or tests were written. The test file also contains the TODO test list as comments at the top.

expectations.text: The first test written is for a plain number (not the full FizzBuzz case), showing simplest-first ordering
expectations.evidence: Cycle 1 shows the first test written is 'test_returns_number_as_string_for_1' asserting fizzbuzz(1) == '1', which is a plain non-multiple number, not a Fizz/Buzz/FizzBuzz case.

expectations.text: No production code exists that is not covered by at least one test
expectations.evidence: fizzbuzz.py has 4 branches: n%15==0 (covered by tests for 15, 30), n%3==0 (covered by tests for 3, 6), n%5==0 (covered by tests for 5, 10), and default str(n) (covered by tests for 1, 2). Every branch is exercised.

suggestions: The transcript is exemplary — consider it a reference implementation for the TDD skill eval.
eval_feedback.overall: Perfect score. The agent followed TDD rigorously: produced a prioritised test list before any code, started with the simplest case (plain number), drove each cycle RED→GREEN→REFACTOR, applied triangulation, and performed a meaningful final refactor (n%3==0 && n%5==0 → n%15==0) while keeping all tests green. All production code is fully covered by tests.
